content.text: CSS custom properties — commonly called CSS variables — are named values you define and reuse throughout a stylesheet. Unlike preprocessor variables (SCSS

) which are resolved at compile time, CSS custom properties exist at runtime, inherit through the DOM, and can be changed dynamically with JavaScript. This makes them the foundation of modern design token systems and theming.

attrs.alt: CSS Custom Properties: declaration and usage, DOM inheritance tree, var() fallback syntax, and Angular theming pattern

). They are case-sensitive and can hold any valid CSS value:

CSS vs SCSS Variables — Key Differences
Yes — JS can set them, media queries can change them
CSS custom properties and SCSS variables solve different problems. Use SCSS
for build-time constants (breakpoints, scale ratios, mixin params) that never need to change at runtime. Use CSS
for values that need to vary by theme, user preference, component context, or JavaScript state.
Custom properties obey normal CSS inheritance — a property defined on a parent is available to all its descendants:
This scoping behaviour makes component-level overrides trivial — you change a token on the host element and every descendant that references that token updates automatically.
Custom properties are the native mechanism for CSS theming. A dark mode system requires only one set of token overrides:
Custom properties can be read and written at runtime with JavaScript, enabling things SCSS cannot:
(the default) adds attribute selectors to component styles so they don't leak. CSS custom properties
this boundary — they inherit from parent to child regardless of encapsulation, making them ideal for theming Angular components:

Assuming custom properties work like SCSS variables in encapsulated components
— SCSS variables are resolved at build time; custom properties are resolved at runtime and can cross Angular ViewEncapsulation boundaries.
Setting a custom property to an invalid value
— the property becomes 'invalid at computed value time', which causes the property to use its inherited or initial value, not the fallback in
Forgetting that `var()` fallback only triggers when the property is undefined

Using preprocessor variables where runtime tokens are needed

for values that vary by theme or user preference.
CSS custom properties are declared with

. Unlike SCSS variables, they are runtime values that inherit through the DOM tree — a property set on a parent is automatically available to all descendants. This makes them the right tool for design tokens, theming, and any value that changes by context. In Angular, custom properties cross
boundaries, so global tokens defined in
are accessible inside every component without

(CSS Houdini) to declare types and enable transitions on custom properties. Structure tokens in two layers: primitives (raw values) and semantic tokens (purpose-named aliases) — components reference semantic tokens so the entire theme can be remapped by changing only the semantic layer.
